title: The Reservoirs

mood.label: Heavy going
mood.summary: The weekend's fragile calm broke hard: the Iran ceasefire collapsed into fresh US strikes on the Strait of Hormuz — including two bombed civilian water reservoirs serving 20,000 people in a fifth-year drought — and Iranian retaliation on US bases, while inflation hit a three-year high of 4.2% and the Dow shed 950 points, and anti-immigrant riots spread out of Belfast across Britain — set against quieter mercies: ocean protection crossing 10%, 603 river barriers removed in Europe, and a photonic chip and cheaper clean hydrogen.

sampleSources: Strudel default library — sampled piano, vibraphone (VCSL), RolandTR808

lines: The pause we were leaning on snapped in the dark, a chopper down by the strait,
and the strikes came back on the islands and ports, then the part I can't set aside —
two reservoirs gone in a fifth year of drought, the water for twenty thousand mouths,
and Tehran says war crime, and the missiles fly back, and somewhere a child went dry.
At home the receipts run hot, four-two on the year, the gas pump up by half,
the Dow drops nine-fifty, the screen bleeds red, and Belfast burns to England's edge.
Still ten percent of the sea is held now, and six hundred barriers torn from the rivers,
a chip that bends its own light into thought — the small good news won't quite leave the ledge.
Carry the reservoirs, carry the names of the water and what it was for,
the day went heavy, it went the wrong way, but I'm not letting go of the rest —
the protected sea, the freed-up streams, the hand that reaches the cut-off town,
hold the loss and the mending at once, and walk through the worst of it slow.
